event.delta: Now I understand the issue. Let me check the default configuration for `mouse_capture`:

I've identified the problem. Here's the analysis:

## Root Cause

The configuration has a **`ui.mouse_capture`** setting that **defaults to `false`** in `crates/common/src/config.rs`:

```rust
fn default_mouse_capture() -> bool {
    false
}
```

This setting controls whether the terminal enables mouse capture mode via the `EnableMouseCapture` ANSI escape sequence.

### Why clicks work but scroll doesn't:

- **Mouse clicks** (`MouseEventKind::Down`/`Up`) work even *without* mouse capture mode — crossterm can still detect mouse button presses
- **Mouse scroll** (`MouseEventKind::ScrollUp`/`ScrollDown`) **requires** mouse capture mode to be enabled — the terminal won't send scroll events to the app without it

### The Fix

Add this to your config file at `~/.dcode-ai/config.toml`:

```toml
[ui]
mouse_capture = true
```

Or you can set it per-workspace in `.dcode-ai/config.local.toml`:

```toml
[ui]
mouse_capture = true
```

Then restart the CLI. Both

mouse selection and scrolling will work.

### Why default is `false`:

Mouse capture disables the terminal's native text selection (you can't highlight text with the mouse anymore), which is intentional — the default is `false` to preserve normal terminal behavior. Users who want mouse scrolling must explicitly opt-in.

I've identified the problem. Here's the analysis:

## Root Cause

The configuration has a **`ui.mouse_capture`** setting that **defaults to `false`** in `crates/common/src/config.rs`:

```rust
fn default_mouse_capture() -> bool {
    false
}
```

This setting controls whether the terminal enables mouse capture mode via the `EnableMouseCapture` ANSI escape sequence.

### Why clicks work but scroll doesn't:

- **Mouse clicks** (`MouseEventKind::Down`/`Up`) work even *without* mouse capture mode — crossterm can still detect mouse button presses
- **Mouse scroll** (`MouseEventKind::ScrollUp`/`ScrollDown`) **requires** mouse capture mode to be enabled — the terminal won't send scroll events to the app without it

### The Fix

Add this to your config file at `~/.dcode-ai/config.toml`:

```toml
[ui]
mouse_capture = true
```

Or you can set it per-workspace in `.dcode-ai/config.local.toml`:

```toml
[ui]
mouse_capture = true
```

Then restart the CLI. Both mouse selection and scrolling will work.

### Why default is `false`:

Mouse capture disables the terminal's native text selection (you can't highlight text with the mouse anymore), which is intentional — the default is `false` to preserve normal terminal behavior. Users who want mouse scrolling must explicitly opt-in.

event.delta:
I found the issue. The **mouse capture is disabled by default** in the config. Looking at line 325 in `crates/common/src/config.rs`:

```rust
fn default_mouse_capture() -> bool {
    false
}
```

When `mouse_capture` is `false`, the TUI doesn't enable mouse capture mode in the terminal, so scroll wheel events are never received.

To fix this, you have two options:

**Option 1: Enable

in your config file** (`~/.dcode-ai/config.toml` or workspace config):
```toml
[ui]
mouse_capture = true
```

**Option 2: I can change the default to `true`** so mouse scrolling works out of the box.

options.label: Enable in config file (set mouse_capture = true)
Change default to true so it works automatically

input.prompt: How would you like to fix the mouse scrolling?

question.prompt: How would you like to fix the mouse scrolling?

options.label: Enable in config file (set mouse_capture = true)
Change default to true so it works automatically
